member.name: Lee Barron
member.role: MP for Corby and East Northamptonshire
member.synopsis: Lee Barron is the Labour MP for Corby and East Northamptonshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Bricks
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether his Department will release guidance on Swift Bricks for new build properties.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Planning Practice Guidance setting out how swift bricks are expected to be used in new developments can be found on gov.uk here.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: British Nationals Abroad: United Arab Emirates
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, how many British citizens resident in the UAE have returned to the UK since the beginning of the war on Iran.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Eggs: Ukraine
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of the temporary removal of tariffs on egg imports from Ukraine on UK egg producers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under our Free Trade Agreement with Ukraine, tariffs on all goods are temporarily removed until March 2029, except for poultry and eggs, where the liberalisation is due to end on 31 March 2028 following the two-year extension announced on 19 January 2026.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Powers of Attorney
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, if he will make an assessment of the rationale for requiring separate applications for a Health and Welfare Lasting Power of Attorney and a Property and Financial Affairs Lasting Power of Attorney, where both pow
writtenQuestions.answerText: A Lasting Power of Attorney (LPA) allows a person (the donor) to choose people they trust (the attorney) to make decisions for them should they lose the mental capacity to make their own decisions. The Mental Capacity Act 2005 provides the legal framework...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Pride in Place Programme: Corby and East Northamptonshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps he is taking to consult with communities on the use of Pride in Place funding in Corby and East Northamptonshire constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The community will decide how to invest £20m of Pride in Place funding in Corby Kingswood. A Neighbourhood Board made up of local people will come up with an investment plan for the future of their area. This plan must evidence how they have consulted the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Debt Collection
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, when his Department will publish the conclusions of the consultation on the regulation of the debt enforcement sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: People in debt deserve to be treated fairly. That is why the Government is supporting the Enforcement Conduct Board’s work to make sure anyone facing enforcement action is treated fairly. We will respond to our consultation about regulation of the enforce...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, if he will publish monthly data from the Capacity Tracker on the number of returned care packages, by (a) NHS-funded and (b) Local Authority-funded recipients.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since January 2025, we have hosted a survey on the Capacity Tracker which has collected information on a voluntary basis from both care home and home care provider locations regarding their handing back of local authority or National Health Service funded...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Incinerators: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if her Department will issue guidance on a reasonable timeframe for the (a) construction and (b) completion of an energy recovery facility with a capacity of 350,000 tonnes per y
writtenQuestions.answerText: All full planning permissions for development, including waste to energy development, are required to be commenced within three years of the grant of permission (unless the local planning authority sets a different time period.) Further guidance on this c...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Almshouses: Affordable Housing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether her Department plans to introduce greater flexibility in the National Planning Policy Framework to support small almshouse charities in building affordable homes.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer to Question UIN 31178 on 24 February 2025.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Sugar Beet
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps he plans to take to support sugar beet growers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: contribution to UK sugar production. Also, that sugar beet itself, used in crop rotations, is beneficial to soil and crop health and allows arable farms a season of “rest” from cereal production. We are committed to promoting fairness across the food supp...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking tackle the shortfall in the children's health and therapy workforce across integrated care systems.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The refreshed Long Term Workforce Plan, which will be published in summer 2025, will deliver the transformed health service we will build over the next decade, to treat patients on time again. We will ensure we have the staff we need so that children and ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Flexible Working and Reasonable Adjustments
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he is taking steps to (a) strengthen the right to flexible working for people with neuro-disabilities and (b) make it the default position for all employers to offer reasonable adjustments for
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Employment Rights Bill contains measures to make flexible working the default for all employees, except where it is not reasonably feasible. A job applicant or employee with cerebral palsy will almost certainly meet the Equality Act 2010's definition ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Suicide: Northamptonshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, how many suicides there were in Northamptonshire in each year since 2020; and how many of these were men.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested falls under the remit of the UK Statistics Authority. A response to the Hon. Gentleman’s Parliamentary Question of 14 January is attached.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Flood Control: Corby and East Northamptonshire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, how much the Environment Agency spent on flood defences in Corby and East Northamptonshire constituency in each year since 2019.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To ensure we protect the country from the devastating impacts of flooding, we will invest £2.4 billion in 2024/25 and 2025/26 to improve flood resilience, by maintaining, repairing and building flood defences. The Environment Agency are best placed to pro...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Carbon Emissions: Technology
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, if he will hold discussions with the electrical contracting industry on estimated future trends in the level of demand for electricians for the implementation of low carbon technologies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Alongside the Clean Power 2030 Action Plan, The Office for Clean Energy Jobs published an evidence annex, which provides an initial assessment of the skills challenges for the Clean Energy Mission. This evidence will be used as a basis for the Government ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Cancer
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, if she will make an assessment of the potential impact of the qualifying period for (a) Disability Living Allowance, (b) Personal Independence Payment and (c) Carers Allowance on applicants under 25 wit
writtenQuestions.answerText: Other than in cases of people nearing the end of life, all claims to either Disability Living Allowance (DLA) or Personal Independence Payment (PIP) are subject to the qualifying period. The qualifying period, together with a prospective test, ensures tha...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent discussions he has had with stakeholders on regulating advertisements of products that are high in (a) fat, (b) salt and (c) sugar (i) on TV and (ii) online.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has engaged with a range of stakeholders on the advertising restrictions for less healthy food or drink, also known as products high in fat, salt or sugar, on television and online. The Department will continue to engage with stakeholders a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Food: Origin Marking
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps his Department is taking to prevent retailers from misrepresenting the origin of (a) meat and (b) dairy products that are not (i) raised and (ii) produced in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK maintains high standards on the information provided on food labels and packaging so that consumers can have confidence in the food that they buy. Country of origin information is required for fresh and frozen meat of beef, sheep, goat, pigs and po...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Transport
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, whether she has had discussions with North Northamptonshire Council on school transport fees.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department’s home to school travel policy aims to make sure that no child is prevented from accessing education by a lack of transport. Local authorities must arrange free home to school travel for children of compulsory school age, between 5 to 16, w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Imports: Companies
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will take steps to help protect British businesses in (a) the hobby sector and (b) other sectors from (i) TEMU and (ii) other cheap import companies.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Under the UK’s Low Value Imports Regime, consignments valued below £135 can be imported into the UK without incurring customs duty. This is a common provision in customs regimes around the world designed to avoid disproportionate burdens on low value trad...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury
